name: Sooper Cu Dba Climb Cu
description_short: NCUA-insured credit union based in Arvada, CO, offering savings accounts, CDs, checking, and IRAs with tiered rate structures. Open membership through Consumers United Association or employer groups.
description_long: Sooper Cu Dba Climb Cu (Climb Credit Union) was established in 1951 and operates as a state-chartered credit union headquartered in Arvada, Colorado with approximately $678.13 million in assets. The institution is NCUA-insured and serves Colorado residents primarily through multiple membership pathways, including affiliation with the Consumers United Association, employer group participation, or geographic eligibility in Arvada, Jefferson County, and select zip codes. The credit union maintains a network of physical branch locations across Colorado. Climb Credit Union offers a comprehensive suite of deposit products including savings accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit (CDs) with varying terms from 6 months to 5 years. Their deposit products feature tiered APY rates that increase with larger deposit amounts—for example, their 12-month CD ranges from 4.75% APY for deposits under $50,000 to 4.85% APY for deposits of $50,000 or more. They also provide checking accounts with the Peak Checking option offering 5.00% APY, variable IRAs, and money market accounts with rates scaling from 3.00% to 4.50% depending on deposit size. What distinguishes Climb Credit Union is their tiered rate structure that rewards larger deposits with incrementally higher returns, their long operational history since 1951, and their open membership model via the Consumers United Association for non-qualifying individuals. The institution is well-capitalized with $77.35 million in capital and maintains healthy loan-to-asset and deposit ratios ($582.76 million in loans against $592.34 million in deposits). However, their savings account rate of 0.10% APY is substantially below current market averages (4.20% comparison rate shown), and their mortgage products currently show no available rates on their platform, suggesting limited mortgage lending activity. Their loan rates section also displays no available options at the time of this profile, indicating potential constraints in consumer lending products beyond their credit-focused offerings. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Money Market accounts with rates from 3.00% to 4.50% APY
6-month CDs with rates from 3.90% to 4.00% APY
12-month CDs with rates from 4.75% to 4.85% APY
Branch banking services at multiple Colorado locations
Online account access and rate comparison tools
pros: Peak Checking account offers 5.00% APY with $0 minimum and no monthly fees
CD rates competitive for larger deposits—12-month CDs reach 4.85% APY on balances of $50,000+
Tiered money market rates scale from 3.00% to 4.50% APY based on deposit size
NCUA insurance protection on all deposits up to standard insurance limits
Open membership pathway via $5 annual Consumers United Association membership for Colorado residents
Substantial asset base of $678.13 million providing institutional stability
Multiple branch locations across Colorado with geographic flexibility
cons: Savings account APY of 0.10% is significantly below market averages (4.20% benchmark shown)
No mortgage rates currently available on platform, limiting home lending options
No consumer loan rates displayed, suggesting limited personal/auto lending products
Membership restricted primarily to Colorado residents and specific geographic/employer criteria
Tiered rate structure requires $50,000+ deposits to access best CD and money market rates
best_for: Colorado residents seeking NCUA-insured deposit products with tiered rates for larger balances
Peak Checking users wanting high-yield checking without minimums or fees
CD investors with $50,000+ to deposit looking for rates above 4.75% APY
Employees of Climb Credit Union's Select Employer Groups with easy membership access
diagnosis: Climb Credit Union is best for Colorado-based savers with substantial deposits ($50,000+) seeking competitive CD and money market rates combined with NCUA insurance protection, or checking account users wanting high-yield checking without fees. The primary caveat is their severely uncompetitive standard savings rate and apparent lack of active mortgage/personal loan products, limiting their utility as a comprehensive financial institution for borrowing needs.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
